prerequisites: A Jamf Pro instance. Note the instance subdomain (the part before .jamfcloud.com in your URL, e.g. "yourcompany" in yourcompany.jamfcloud.com), which maps to the connector's subdomain field.
A Jamf Pro user account whose username and password the connector will use to authenticate (maps to the username and password fields).
Read (GET) privileges granted to that user account in Jamf Pro for the objects you want to extract, since Classic API privileges align with the account's configured permissions.

authMethods.name: Basic authentication (Jamf Pro username and password)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates to the Jamf Pro Classic API with a Jamf Pro user account's username and password (the connector's username and password fields). API access is limited to the privileges configured on that account. Note: Jamf has restricted Basic Authentication on newer Jamf Pro versions, where username and password may only be used to obtain a bearer token rather than for direct endpoint calls. Confirm your Jamf Pro version supports the credentials this connector provides.

setupSteps.title: Find your Jamf Pro subdomain
setupSteps.body: Open your Jamf Pro instance and look at its URL. For a Jamf Cloud instance this has the form https://yourcompany.jamfcloud.com. Enter the subdomain portion (for example yourcompany) into the connector's subdomain field.

setupSteps.title: Create or choose a Jamf Pro user account
setupSteps.body: In Jamf Pro, use an existing user account or create a dedicated one under Settings > User accounts and groups. Grant it the Read (GET) privileges needed for the objects you want to extract. Enter its username in the connector's username field.

setupSteps.title: Provide the account password
setupSteps.body: Enter the password for the selected Jamf Pro user account into the connector's password (secret) field. The connector uses this username and password to authenticate to the Classic API.

sources.title: Classic API Authentication Changes - Jamf Developer Portal
